# AUTO-GENERATED by split_acceptance_tests.py
# Each original test function was split into one function per top-level
# assertion so pytest reports per-check pass/fail counts.
import re

def test_argmin_argmax_dtype_precedence_order_matches_fix__a1():
    source = open('/workspace/cupy/core/_routines_statistics.pyx', 'r', encoding='utf-8').read()
    expected = "for r in 'qlihb' for d in '?BhHiIlLqQ'"
    unexpected = "for r in 'bhilq' for d in '?BhHiIlLqQ'"
    assert expected in source, "Expected _argmin/_argmax dtype precedence order to be fixed to 'qlihb' (so int64 'q' is preferred when dtype=None), but the fixed pattern was not found in /workspace/cupy/core/_routines_statistics.pyx."

def test_argmin_argmax_dtype_precedence_order_matches_fix__a2():
    source = open('/workspace/cupy/core/_routines_statistics.pyx', 'r', encoding='utf-8').read()
    expected = "for r in 'qlihb' for d in '?BhHiIlLqQ'"
    unexpected = "for r in 'bhilq' for d in '?BhHiIlLqQ'"
    assert unexpected not in source, "Found the old buggy dtype precedence order 'bhilq' in /workspace/cupy/core/_routines_statistics.pyx; this would pick a smaller integer return type than intended when dtype=None."

def test_argmin_argmax_dtype_precedence_order_matches_fix__a3():
    source = open('/workspace/cupy/core/_routines_statistics.pyx', 'r', encoding='utf-8').read()
    expected = "for r in 'qlihb' for d in '?BhHiIlLqQ'"
    unexpected = "for r in 'bhilq' for d in '?BhHiIlLqQ'"
    n = len(re.findall(re.escape(expected), source))
    assert n >= 2, f'Expected the fixed dtype precedence generator clause to appear at least twice (for both _argmin and _argmax), but found it only {n} time(s).'

}
